q: Which property allows metals to be beaten into thin sheets?
sol: Malleability is the property of metals by which they can be beaten into thin sheets like silver foil.

q: Which of these is a non-metal that is liquid at room temperature?
sol: Bromine is the only non-metal that exists in liquid state at room temperature.

q: Metals produce a ringing sound when struck, so they are called:
sol: Sonorous means producing a deep or ringing sound.

q: The property of drawing metals into thin wires is called:
sol: Ductility allows metals like gold and copper to be drawn into long wires.

q: Which metal is stored in kerosene due to its high reactivity?
sol: Sodium and Potassium are highly reactive and catch fire when exposed to air or moisture.

q: Non-metals generally react with oxygen to form ________ oxides.
sol: Non-metallic oxides (like SO2) dissolve in water to form acids.

q: Which non-metal is essential for our life and is inhaled during breathing?
sol: Oxygen is a non-metal required by all living beings for respiration.

q: What happens when Copper is exposed to moist air for a long time?
sol: The green coating is a mixture of copper hydroxide and copper carbonate.

q: Which of the following is the best conductor of electricity?
sol: Silver has the highest electrical conductivity among all metals.

q: Phosphorus is a very reactive non-metal. To prevent its contact with air, it is stored in:
sol: Phosphorus catches fire in air but does not react with water.

q: Generally, metallic oxides are ________ in nature.
sol: Metallic oxides (like MgO) react with water to form bases (Mg(OH)2).

q: Which of these is a non-metal but is lustrous (shiny)?
sol: Iodine is an exception among non-metals as it has a natural shine.

q: The reaction in which a more reactive metal replaces a less reactive metal is called:
sol: Example: Zinc + Copper Sulphate -> Zinc Sulphate + Copper.

q: Which metal is found in liquid state at room temperature?
sol: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at normal room temperature.

q: Which non-metal is used in purple-colored antiseptic solutions on wounds?
sol: Tincture of iodine is used as a powerful antiseptic.

q: What gas is produced when metals react with dilute acids?
sol: Metals react with acids to produce salt and hydrogen gas (with a 'pop' sound).

q: Which of the following can be beaten into thin sheets?
sol: Zinc is a metal, while others are non-metals (which are brittle).

q: The sound produced by a school bell is due to which property of metals?
sol: Sonority is the ability to produce a ringing sound.

q: Which of these is used in making jewelry because it is least reactive?
sol: Gold is a 'noble metal' that doesn't tarnish or react easily.

q: Non-metals are generally ________ conductors of heat and electricity.
sol: Non-metals lack free electrons, making them poor conductors.

q: Which non-metal is used in crackers (fireworks)?
sol: Sulfur and Phosphorus are used in the manufacturing of crackers.

q: Magnesium + Oxygen → ?
sol: Burning magnesium ribbon produces white ash of Magnesium Oxide (MgO).

q: A solution of Magnesium oxide turns ________ litmus ________.
sol: MgO is basic, so it turns red litmus blue.

q: Which metal will displace copper from copper sulphate solution?
sol: Iron is more reactive than copper, so it displaces it.

q: The non-metal used in water purification to kill germs is:
sol: Chlorination is a common method for disinfecting water.

q: Which of these is a property of non-metals?
sol: Non-metals break into pieces when hammered; they are brittle.

q: Graphite is an allotrope of which non-metal?
sol: Graphite and Diamond are forms of Carbon.

q: Which non-metal is a good conductor of electricity?
sol: Graphite is an exceptional non-metal that conducts electricity.

q: Which metal is used in thermometers?
sol: Mercury is used because it stays liquid and expands uniformly with heat.

q: What happens when Sulfur dioxide reacts with water?
sol: SO2 + H2O -> H2SO3 (Sulfurous acid).

q: Copper does not react with ________ even on heating.
sol: Copper is less reactive and doesn't displace hydrogen from dilute HCl.

q: Which of these metals can be cut with a knife?
sol: Sodium and Potassium are soft metals that can be sliced easily.

q: Iron + Copper Sulphate → Iron Sulphate + Copper. This shows iron is ________ than copper.
sol: Displacement occurs only if the displacing metal is more reactive.

q: Aluminum foils are used for wrapping food because aluminum is:
sol: Aluminum can be made into very thin foils due to high malleability.

q: Which non-metal is used as a fertilizer for plants?
sol: Nitrogen and Phosphorus are key components of fertilizers.

q: The reaction of Sodium with water is:
sol: Sodium reacts violently with water, producing a lot of heat.

q: Metals react with Sodium hydroxide to produce:
sol: Reactive metals like Zinc or Aluminum react with bases to release H2.

q: Why can't we store lemon pickle in an aluminum utensil?
o: Acid in lemon reacts with aluminum
sol: Acids react with metals to form toxic salts, which can spoil the food.

q: Which of these is a metalloid (shows properties of both)?
sol: Silicon, Germanium, etc., are metalloids.

q: What is the color of Iron Sulphate solution?
sol: Iron sulphate (FeSO4) is pale green in color.

q: Non-metals do not react with:
sol: Generally, non-metals do not react with water, which is why Phosphorus is stored in it.

q: Which of the following will show a displacement reaction?
sol: Zinc is more reactive than Copper, so it will displace it.

q: The 'Pop' sound test is used for which gas?
sol: Hydrogen gas burns with a characteristic pop sound.

q: Sulfur is used in the process of ________ of rubber.
sol: Vulcanization makes rubber stronger and more durable.

q: Which metal is used in galvanization to prevent rusting of iron?
sol: Zinc coating protects iron from air and moisture.

q: Metals are generally ________ at room temperature (except Mercury).
sol: Most metals have high melting points and are solid.

q: Rusting of iron is a reaction of iron with:
sol: Both air and water are necessary for the formation of rust (Hydrated Iron Oxide).

q: Which of these is NOT a property of metals?
sol: Metals are actually excellent conductors.

q: Charcoal is a form of:
sol: Charcoal is an impure form of the non-metal Carbon.

q: What is the formula of Copper Sulphate?
sol: CuSO4 is the chemical formula for Copper (II) Sulphate.

q: Gold is used for making coins and medals because it is:
sol: Its stability and beauty make it ideal for precious items.

q: Which metal is used in electric wires?
sol: Copper is used due to its high conductivity and ductility.

q: Metals ________ when hammered.
sol: This is due to malleability.

q: Zinc is ________ reactive than Iron.
sol: In the reactivity series, Zinc is placed above Iron.

q: Which of the following non-metals is used in the manufacture of steel?
sol: Carbon is mixed with iron to make it hard and form steel.

q: Oxides of Sulfur and Nitrogen cause:
sol: These oxides react with rainwater to form acids.

q: Magnesium reacts with hot water to form:
sol: Mg + 2H2O -> Mg(OH)2 + H2.

q: Which metal is essential in the human body for blood (hemoglobin)?
sol: Iron deficiency leads to anemia.

q: Non-metals are ________ when they are in solid form.
sol: Non-metals typically have a non-lustrous, dull appearance.

q: Copper + Zinc Sulphate → ?
sol: No reaction occurs because Copper is less reactive than Zinc.

q: The property of metals to be hard is called:
sol: Most metals (except Na, K, Hg) are hard to touch.

q: Which non-metal is used to preserve food items?
sol: Nitrogen gas is used to flush food packets (like chips) to prevent oxidation.

q: Metals have ________ melting and boiling points.
sol: Strong metallic bonds require a lot of energy to break.

q: What is the color of Copper Sulphate solution?
sol: Copper sulphate is also known as blue vitriol.

q: Which of these is a metal but is not hard?
sol: Potassium is a soft metal like Sodium.

q: Non-metals react with ________ to form negative ions.
sol: Non-metals are electronegative in nature.

q: The chemical name of rust is:
sol: Rust is Fe2O3.xH2O.

q: Which of the following will react with cold water?
sol: Sodium is so reactive it reacts with cold water instantly.

q: Sulfur is a ________ at room temperature.
sol: Sulfur is a yellow brittle solid.

q: Displacement reactions depend on the ________ of metals.
sol: The reactivity series determines which metal can displace another.

q: Which of these is used to make lead of pencils?
sol: Pencil 'lead' is actually a mixture of graphite and clay.

q: Gold, Silver, and Platinum are called:
sol: They are called noble because they are very unreactive.

q: When zinc reacts with sulfuric acid, it produces:
sol: Zn + H2SO4 -> ZnSO4 + H2.

q: Which of these is a use of metals?
sol: Metals are vital for modern industry and technology.

q: Metals generally have ________ density.
sol: Metals are heavy for their volume because atoms are closely packed.

q: Bronze is an alloy of Copper and:
sol: Bronze = Copper + Tin.

q: Which non-metal is used to convert vegetable oil into vegetable ghee?
sol: The process is called hydrogenation.

q: Copper vessels get corroded to form a ________ layer.
sol: This is due to the formation of basic copper carbonate.

q: Which metal is used in the construction of bridges and buildings?
sol: Iron (in the form of steel) is used for its strength.

q: Non-metals ________ when hit with a hammer.
sol: Non-metals are brittle and lack malleability.

q: Which metal reacts with steam but not with cold or hot water?
sol: Iron is less reactive than Na or Mg.

q: What is the nature of the oxide formed by burning phosphorus?
sol: P2O5 reacts with water to form phosphoric acid.

q: Which of these is a characteristic of most metals?
sol: Most metals reflect light from their surface (lustre).

q: Sulfuric acid is produced when ________ is added to water.
sol: SO3 + H2O -> H2SO4.

q: Which metal is used for electroplating to give a shiny look to bicycle parts?
sol: Chromium plating provides shine and resists scratches.

q: The word 'Metal' is derived from a Greek word 'Metallon', which means:
sol: It originally referred to the process of mining.

q: Which non-metal is found in every living thing?
sol: Carbon is the basis of all organic life molecules.

q: Metals displace hydrogen from bases ________.
sol: Only certain metals like Zn, Al react with bases like NaOH.

q: Mercury is a ________ metal.
sol: Its liquid state at room temperature is a unique property.

q: What happens when iron nails are placed in copper sulphate solution?
o: Blue color fades and becomes green
sol: Iron displaces copper, turning the solution green (FeSO4).

q: Metals generally form ________ when they react with chlorine.
sol: Metallic chlorides are usually solid salts.

q: Which of these is a noble gas (non-reactive non-metal)?
sol: Helium belongs to the noble gas group.

q: Silicon is used in making:
sol: Silicon is a semi-conductor (metalloid).

q: Metals react with oxygen to form ________.
sol: The chemical combination results in metallic oxides.

q: Why is copper used for making boilers?
o: It is a good conductor of heat
sol: Its high thermal conductivity is efficient for heating water.

q: A non-metal that is used to disinfect swimming pools is:
sol: Chlorine is effective at killing water-borne pathogens.

q: Reactivity of metals ________ down the activity series.
sol: Potassium is at the top (most reactive), Gold at the bottom.

q: Which of these is the most ductile metal?
sol: 1 gram of gold can be drawn into a 2 km long wire.

q: Alloying of metals is done to:
sol: Alloys like stainless steel are superior to pure metals in many ways.

q: Metals can be distinguished from non-metals based on ________ properties.
sol: Both sets of properties show distinct differences between them.
